meta.summary: Seqtk fast lightweight toolkit for processing FASTA and FASTQ sequences.
meta.description: Seqtk fast lightweight toolkit for processing FASTA and FASTQ sequences.

test: { seqtk 2>&1 || true; } | grep -F 'Version: 1.5-r133' >/dev/null && { seqtk sample 2>&1 || true; } | grep -F 'Usage:   seqtk sample' >/dev/null && ldd $(command -v seqtk) | grep -F 'libz.so' >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-seqtk-fastx && cd /tmp/taf-seqtk-fastx && printf '@r1
ACGTNN
+
FFFFFF
@r2
TTAGGGTTAGGG
+
FFFFFFFFFFFF
' > reads.fq && gzip -c reads.fq > reads.fq.gz && seqtk seq -a reads.fq.gz > reads.fa && grep -F '>r1' reads.fa >/dev/null && grep -F 'TTAGGGTTAGGG' reads.fa >/dev/null && printf '>chr1
ACGTACGTNNNNACGT
>chr2
TTAGGGTTAGGGCCCTAA
' > ref.fa && seqtk seq -R ref.fa > both.fa && grep -F '>chr1+' both.fa >/dev/null && grep -F '>chr1-' both.fa >/dev/null && seqtk size ref.fa > size.txt && grep -F "$(printf '2	34')" size.txt >/dev/null && seqtk comp ref.fa > comp.txt && grep -F "$(printf 'chr1	16')" comp.txt >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-seqtk-subseq && cd /tmp/taf-seqtk-subseq && printf '@r1
ACGTNN
+
FFFFFF
@r2
TTAGGGTTAGGG
+
FFFFFFFFFFFF
' > reads.fq && printf '>chr1
ACGTACGTNNNNACGT
>chr2
TTAGGGTTAGGGCCCTAA
' > ref.fa && printf 'r1
' > names.txt && printf 'chr1	4	12
' > regions.bed && seqtk subseq reads.fq names.txt > r1.fq && grep -F '@r1' r1.fq >/dev/null && ! grep -F '@r2' r1.fq >/dev/null && seqtk subseq ref.fa regions.bed > sub.fa && grep -F '>chr1:5-12' sub.fa >/dev/null && seqtk seq -M regions.bed ref.fa > masked.fa && grep -F 'ACGTacgtnnnnACGT' masked.fa >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-seqtk-qc && cd /tmp/taf-seqtk-qc && printf '@r1
ACGTNN
+
FFFFFF
@r2
TTAGGGTTAGGG
+
FFFFFFFFFFFF
' > reads.fq && seqtk trimfq -b 1 -e 1 reads.fq > trim.fq && grep -F 'CGTN' trim.fq >/dev/null && grep -F 'TAGGGTTAGG' trim.fq >/dev/null && seqtk fqchk reads.fq > fqchk.txt && grep -F 'avg_len: 9.00' fqchk.txt >/dev/null && seqtk sample -s7 reads.fq 1 > sample.fq && grep -F '@r1' sample.fq >/dev/null

mkdir -p /tmp/taf-seqtk-extra && cd /tmp/taf-seqtk-extra && printf '>chr1
ACGTACGTNNNNACGT
>chr2
TTAGGGTTAGGGCCCTAA
' > ref.fa && seqtk hpc ref.fa > hpc.fa && grep -F 'ACGTACGTNACGT' hpc.fa >/dev/null && seqtk gap -l 2 ref.fa > gap.bed && grep -F "$(printf 'chr1	8	12')" gap.bed >/dev/null && seqtk split -n 2 chunk ref.fa && test -s chunk.00001.fa && test -s chunk.00002.fa && seqtk telo -P ref.fa > telo.bed 2> telo.count && grep -F "$(printf '0	34')" telo.count >/dev/null
